title: pcf-casoratian-catalogue: A machine-verified higher-order (order ≥ 3, uniform in k) Casoratian law for linear recurrences — the Abel–Jacobi–Liouville identity C(n+1) = (−1)^(k−1) c₀(n) C(n) in Lean 4 / Mathlib
description:
The higher-order (order ≥ 3) Casoratian law for a homogeneous linear recurrence, formalized to a clean axiom cone in Lean 4 / Mathlib. For the order-k recurrence s(n+k) = ∑j=0k−1 cj(n) s(n+j), the order-k Casoratian (the k×k discrete Wronskian of k solutions) C(n) = det[ s(t)(n+i) ]0≤i,t<k obeys the first-order Abel–Jacobi–Liouville law C(n+1) = (−1)k−1 c0(n) C(n), hence the closed product form C(n) = ( ∏m<n (−1)k−1 c0(m) ) C(0). Only the lowest coefficient c0 survives; c1 … ck−1 cancel. At k = 2 the sign is the corpus −c0; at k = 3 it flips to +c0. The (−1)k−1 sign is now established as a single uniform theorem over all k, not merely the k = 2, 3 instances.

Why it matters. The deposited corpus formalized only order-2 (three-term) Casoratians; this is the first order-≥3 result. The higher-order Casoratian is the algebraic backbone of simultaneous / Hermite–Padé approximation — the engine of Apéry-type irrationality proofs.

Contents (catalogue entry C1). Nine Lean theorems across two namespaces. The uniform-in-k core (namespace PcfGeneralCaso, GeneralCaso.lean): casoMat_det_step (the general step C(n+1) = (−1)k c0(n) C(n) for the order-(k+1) Casoratian, proved by rewriting the last row via the recurrence into a combination of the cyclically rotated rows of C(n) — Matrix.det_updateRow_sum collapses it to the c0 coefficient and sign_finRotate supplies the cyclic sign), casoMat_det_eq (uniform closed product form), casoMat_det_eq_const (constant-c0 power form), and casoMat_det_ne_zero_of_init (integral-domain non-degeneracy at all k). The by-hand low-order witnesses (namespace PcfHigherCaso): caso3_step (the order-3 step law), caso3_eq (closed product form), caso3_eq_const (constant-c0 case C(n)=c0n C(0)), caso3_ne_zero_of_init (a non-degeneracy / linear-independence certificate over an integral domain), and caso2_step (the order-2 sign −c0 recovered as a faithfulness witness). A sympy gate (harness_caso_k.py): a fully symbolic proof at k=3 plus exact-rational verification k=2..7 plus named instances (the Tribonacci recurrence c0≡1 → constant Casoratian; c0(n)=n+1 → C(n)=n! C(0)).

Epistemic status (SIARC four-class) — PROVEN. Each of the nine theorems was rechecked from source with lake env lean Check.lean under the pinned leanprover/lean4:v4.30.0 + Mathlib v4.30.0 toolchain: every axiom cone is a subset of {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound} (the two _step lemmas need only {propext, Quot.sound}), with no sorryAx and no sorry/admit/native_decide in source. The Lean core is built and cone-checked inside the companion pcf-delta PcfContinuant project, where it is cross-listed in Check.lean alongside the order-2 general Casoratian (casoratian_eqG).

Open / next (CONJECTURED). A concrete simultaneous-Hermite–Padé PCF whose order-3 Casoratian furnishes an Apéry-style irrationality certificate. The (−1)k−1 uniform-k sign theorem, previously listed open here, is now PROVEN (casoMat_det_step; clean cone, gate-checked for all k).
notes: ORCID (0009-0000-6192-8273) is source-verified. The version/concept DOIs will be minted by hand at deposit time. RELATED IDENTIFIERS (all verified live via doi.org 302 redirect to zenodo.org on 2026-06-10, not asserted from memory): (continues) 10.5281/zenodo.20500490 -- the order-2 Casoratian Lean formalization (Zenodo API resource_type software, version record 20500491, title 'Machine-verified Lean 4 / Mathlib formalization of the Casoratian closed form (Theorem 6.3) for a 4/pi polynomial continued fraction', creator ORCID 0009-0000-6192-8273); C1 is the order->=3 extension of this order-2 result. (isSupplementTo) 10.5281/zenodo.19491767 -- the deposited ladder paper 'Polynomial Continued Fractions: A Proved Logarithmic Ladder, a 4/pi Casoratian Identity, and 482 Irrational Constants' (version record 19491768, resource_type publication). (isPartOf) 10.5281/zenodo.20578400 -- companion pcf-delta, which hosts and builds the Lean core (version record 20593930, resource_type publication/preprint). (isPartOf) 10.5281/zenodo.19885549 -- the SIARC program statement (version record 20114861, resource_type publication/preprint). GATE STATUS (confirmed from source, not asserted from memory): on 2026-06-10 the nine theorems -- PcfHigherCaso.caso2_step, caso3_step, caso3_eq, caso3_eq_const, caso3_ne_zero_of_init and PcfGeneralCaso.casoMat_det_step, casoMat_det_eq, casoMat_det_eq_const, casoMat_det_ne_zero_of_init -- were rechecked with 'lake env lean Check.lean' in the pcf-delta PcfContinuant project (toolchain leanprover/lean4:v4.30.0, Mathlib v4.30.0; lake build exit 0, 1975 jobs); all axiom cones are subsets of {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound} (the order-2/3 _step lemmas: {propext, Quot.sound}), no sorryAx, and verify/HigherCaso.lean and verify/GeneralCaso.lean contain no sorry/admit/native_decide. PcfGeneralCaso.casoMat_det_step is the uniform-in-k general-order law C(n+1)=(-1)^k c_0(n) C(n) for the order-(k+1) Casoratian, promoting the previously-open general-k sign item to PROVEN. NOVELTY: a scan of the depositor's 36 public Zenodo records on 2026-06-10 found no order->=3 / higher-order Casoratian, Hermite-Pade, simultaneous-approximation, or Wronskian-of-three deposit; the only Casoratian deposits are order-2 (10.5281/zenodo.20500490 and the ladder paper 10.5281/zenodo.19491767). Public GitHub repo https://github.com/papanokechi/pcf-casoratian-catalogue; this .zenodo.json is documentation only and is not wired to a release webhook.
